sections: One Family, One Phone Number — Every Stage of Your Move

Most people picture a removal as a single van turning up on the day. In reality a well-run move is a chain of linked tasks — surveying, planning, packing, loading, transporting, storing and unpacking — and the weak link is usually the moment one company hands you over to another. At Wolves Removals we deliberately keep the whole chain in-house so there is no finger-pointing and no gap for things to fall through. As a family-run Sussex removals & storage company based at Doryln House, London Road, Ashington, near Pulborough (RH20 3JT), and trading since 2016, we have built our reputation on being the one number you ring for everything. Whether you need a full house removals service, a fast man and van for a smaller load, or a complex office relocation, you deal with the same firm from first enquiry to final box.

That continuity matters more than people expect. When a single team owns your move, the surveyor’s notes feed the packer’s plan, the packer’s plan feeds the loading order, and the loading order feeds the unload at the other end. Nothing is re-explained, nothing is lost in translation, and your dedicated move coordinator — assigned to every job we take — can answer any question because they can see the whole picture, not just one slice of it.
How a Fully Managed Move Actually Works, Step by Step

A managed move is not a mystery, but it does follow a deliberate order. Knowing the sequence in advance removes most of the stress, so here is exactly how we run a typical job from the day you first call us on 01903 893731 to the moment you sit down in your new home.

  • Survey & quote — we assess the volume, access, parking and any tricky items, either at your home or by a guided video walk-through, then issue a clear written quotation with no hidden extras.
  • Move plan & date lock — your coordinator confirms the date, the crew size, the right vehicle and any packing or storage you have chosen, and flags anything that needs special handling.
  • Packing — we supply materials and, if you have booked our full packing service, wrap and box everything room by room with labelled cartons.
  • Load day — the crew protects floors and doorways, loads in a planned order so the unload mirrors your new layout, and inventories the consignment.
  • Transport — whether it is across the village or across the Channel, your goods travel secured and tracked by a team that knows the route.
  • Unload, place & reassemble — furniture goes into the right rooms, beds and flat-pack are rebuilt, and we clear away the packing debris before we leave.

Because the same firm carries that plan from start to finish, you are never asked to repeat yourself and nothing slips between contractors.
Matching the Right Service to Your Situation

“Removals” covers a wide spread of needs, and choosing the right service is half the battle. The point of offering the full range under one roof is that we can steer you to the option that genuinely fits — rather than forcing every job into one fixed package. Here is how our services tend to line up with real-world situations.

  • Moving home — a standard chain move, a first flat, or a large family house all sit under our home removals, with crew and van scaled to the property.
  • Small or single-item loads — a studio flat, a few rooms of furniture or a single bulky delivery suit our flexible man and van service.
  • Businesses on the move — offices, shops and premises that need out-of-hours working and minimal downtime are handled by our commercial removals team.
  • Moving abroad or across the Continent — relocations to France, Spain and beyond run through our European removals and wider international removals services.
  • Students — term-time and end-of-year moves between halls, digs and home are covered by our budget-friendly student removals.

If you are not sure which one applies, that is precisely what your coordinator is for — tell us what you are moving and from where, and we will point you to the right service.
The Survey & Quote: Where a Good Move Is Won

Every reliable removal starts with an honest assessment, because the quote is only as accurate as the survey behind it. Cheap quotes given over the phone with no idea of your volume are the single biggest cause of move-day surprises — the van turns out too small, the crew too few, or the price suddenly climbs. We do it the other way round. Before we put a figure to your job we look at how much you are moving, how the access works at both ends, whether the lorry can park near the door, and whether anything needs specialist care.

That survey can be done in person or by a guided video call, whichever suits you, and it lets us recommend the correct crew, the correct vehicle and the correct amount of time. The result is a written, itemised quotation you can actually rely on. As a fully insured firm carrying £10m public liability cover, and as a LAPADA member and Checkatrade-verified business recommended by estate agents including Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart, we are accountable for the figures we give you. When you are ready to start, you can request a tailored removals quote or read more about our transparent removals pricing first.
Beyond the Standard Move: Storage and Specialist Handling

A good removals firm has to do more than carry boxes from A to B, because real moves rarely run in a clean straight line. Completion dates slip, downsizers need somewhere to keep the overflow, and some possessions are simply too precious or too awkward for an ordinary crew. Keeping these extras under the same roof means you never have to find — and trust — a separate company at the worst possible moment.

Our secure storage solutions bridge the gap when your move-in and move-out dates do not match, or when you are decluttering before a sale. For finer pieces, our experience as a LAPADA member underpins our specialist antiques moving, where bespoke wrapping and careful handling protect period furniture, artwork and heirlooms. And for one of the most demanding items in any home, our trained piano moving service handles uprights and grands with the right equipment and technique. Because all of this sits within the same managed move, your antiques, your piano and your stored goods are all logged on one inventory and overseen by one coordinator.
Why One Provider for the Whole Move Pays Off

The case for a single, accountable provider comes down to three things: continuity, accountability and value. Continuity means the knowledge gathered at survey flows all the way through to the unload, so no one re-learns your move from scratch. Accountability means there is one firm responsible for every item — if you have a question on the day, the person who answers can act on it, rather than blaming an unseen subcontractor. And value comes from combining services sensibly: packing, transport and storage planned together are almost always smoother and better priced than three separate bookings stitched together by you.

As a Sussex family business covering West and East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent — plus moves across the UK and into Europe — we have spent since 2016 refining exactly this one-stop approach. You can learn more about our family-run team and values, or simply call 01903 893731 or email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk and let one coordinator take the whole thing off your hands.
faqs: Do I have to book every part of my move, or can you arrange it all together?

You only ever speak to us. Once we understand your move, your dedicated coordinator assembles the right combination of survey, packing, transport, storage and specialist handling and runs it as a single managed job. You will not be passed to a separate packing firm, storage company or overseas agent — it is all kept in-house under one point of contact, with one written quotation.
How do I know which removal service is right for me?

Tell us what you are moving and from where, and we will steer you to the right option — a full house removal for a property move, a flexible man and van for a smaller load, commercial removals for a business, or our European and international removals for moves abroad. The survey makes the recommendation precise, so you never pay for more than you need.
Can you store my belongings if my completion dates don’t line up?

Yes. Our secure storage is designed for exactly this — when your move-out and move-in dates do not match, or when you are downsizing and need somewhere to keep the overflow. Because storage is part of the same managed move, your goods stay on one inventory and under the care of the same coordinator throughout.
Are you properly insured and accredited?

We are fully insured, carrying £10m public liability cover, and we are a LAPADA member and Checkatrade-verified business recommended by estate agents including Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart. To start your move you can request a tailored quote or look at our pricing first.
